name: Pulumi MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Pulumi MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Pulumi's infrastructure-as-code platform — deploying stacks, querying resource state, managing cloud infrastructure, and accessing Pulumi AI documentation.
use_cases: Querying Pulumi stack state for current infrastructure overview
Deploying infrastructure updates from agent-driven IaC workflows
Listing stack outputs for configuration values in agent workflows
Checking Pulumi Cloud deployment history and status
AI-assisted infrastructure generation using Pulumi AI
not_for: Teams using Terraform, CDK, or other IaC tools — use those integrations
Direct cloud provider API calls (use cloud provider MCPs instead)
Pulumi is a programming model — not suitable for one-off infrastructure queries
best_when: An agent needs to deploy, update, or query infrastructure defined in Pulumi stacks — especially when used alongside Pulumi AI for code generation.
avoid_when: You're using Terraform, AWS CDK, or other IaC tools — Pulumi-specific tooling won't help.

auth.notes: Pulumi Cloud API tokens with team-based access controls. OIDC for CI/CD environments. Personal tokens for individual agents. Team tokens for shared agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Individual: free forever for 1 user; Team: 1 month free trial
pricing.notes: Pulumi is free for individual use. MCP server is open source. Team features require paid plan.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not prominently documented

known_agent_gotchas: Pulumi operations can be long-running (minutes) — agents must handle async deployment status
Stack state is stored in Pulumi Cloud — operations require network access to state backend
Deployment previews (dry runs) should be run before applies in agent workflows
Cloud provider credentials must be configured separately from Pulumi credentials
Stack outputs can contain sensitive values — agents should not log these
Multi-stack deployments require careful ordering — dependency management is complex
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API tokens with team-based access. OIDC for keyless CI/CD. Stack state stored encrypted in Pulumi Cloud. SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Token-based auth with team access controls. OIDC for CI/CD. Encrypted state storage. SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001.
